title: Add riscv64 to cross-compilation CI target
body:
The `local-cross` Makefile target currently cross-compiles for darwin/amd64, linux/arm, linux/arm64, windows/386, and windows/amd64, but not linux/riscv64.

Adding riscv64 would validate that skopeo compiles for RISC-V on every PR, catching architecture-specific regressions. This is the same approach recently accepted for Podman (containers/podman#28333, approved by @Luap99).

Skopeo builds and runs on native riscv64 hardware with no source changes:

```
$ ./bin/skopeo --version
skopeo version 1.22.0-dev

$ file bin/skopeo
ELF 64-bit LSB executable, UCB RISC-V, RVC, double-float ABI
```

Built on BananaPi F3 (SpacemiT K1, rv64gc).

Happy to submit a PR adding `bin/skopeo.linux.riscv64` to the `local-cross` target.
